DispatchConditionRemarks.Para: One in each system may be inoperative provided:

ListItem.Para: Inoperative pump pressure transducer is deactivated.
Associated pump is verified to operate normally before each departure.
Associated pump temperature indications operate normally.
Associated hydraulic system pressure transducer operates normally.

Note.Para: Hydraulic system pressure transducers and hydraulic pump pressure transducers are interchangeable.

MaintenanceProcedure.Para: Deactivate the inoperative pump pressure transducer, and verify the hydraulic pump associated with the inoperative pump pressure indication is operating normally (AMM DDG 29-31-02).

ListItem.Para: For the left EDP inoperative hydraulic pump pressure transducer:
Using a flight deck multi-function display only, open circuit breaker HYD PRESS SNSR-CHAN1 and lock with a DO NOT CLOSE tag.
Gain access to the inoperative hydraulic pump pressure transducer.
Disconnect, cap and stow the associated transducer electrical connector.
Close circuit breaker HYD PRESS SNSR-CHAN1.
For the left EMP inoperative hydraulic pump pressure transducer:
Using a flight deck multi-function display only, open circuit breaker MEDC CHAN B L ENGINE and lock with a DO NOT CLOSE tag.
Gain access to the inoperative hydraulic pump pressure transducer.
Disconnect, cap and stow the associated transducer electrical connector.
Close circuit breaker MEDC CHAN B L ENGINE.
For the right EDP inoperative hydraulic pump pressure transducer:
Using a flight deck multi-function display only, open circuit breaker HYD PRESS SNSR-CHAN2 and lock with a DO NOT CLOSE tag.
Gain access to the inoperative hydraulic pump pressure transducer.
Disconnect, cap and stow the associated transducer electrical connector.
Close circuit breaker HYD PRESS SNSR-CHAN2.
For the right EMP inoperative hydraulic pump pressure transducer:
Using a flight deck multi-function display only, open circuit breaker MEDC CHAN B R ENGINE and lock with a DO NOT CLOSE tag.
Gain access to the inoperative hydraulic pump pressure transducer.
Disconnect, cap and stow the associated transducer electrical connector.
Close circuit breaker MEDC CHAN B R ENGINE.
For the center C1 inoperative hydraulic pump pressure transducer:
Using a flight deck multi-function display only, open circuit breaker HYD PRESS SNSR-CHAN3 and lock with a DO NOT CLOSE tag.
Gain access to the inoperative hydraulic pump pressure transducer.
Disconnect, cap and stow the associated transducer electrical connector.
Close circuit breaker HYD PRESS SNSR-CHAN3.
For the center C2 inoperative hydraulic pump pressure transducer:
Using a flight deck multi-function display only, open circuit breaker HYD PRESS SNSR-CHAN2 and lock with a DO NOT CLOSE tag.
Gain access to the inoperative hydraulic pump pressure transducer.
Disconnect, cap and stow the associated transducer electrical connector.
Close circuit breaker HYD PRESS SNSR-CHAN2.

Para.rev:revHighlight: Revised (M) step 7 to provide further clarification on how the system operates.

ListItem.Para: Ensure there is sufficient ground power for operation of the EMP (Electric Motor Pump).
If the inoperative pressure indication system is for an engine-driven pump, do one of the following:
Dry motor the engine.
Start the engine.
Set the associated hydraulic pump switch to ON for at least 60 seconds.

For the hydraulic system associated with the inoperative pump pressure indication, verify the system pressure indication on the HYDRAULIC maintenance page 1 of 2 is greater than 4700 psi (3000 psi if dry motoring the engine for an engine-driven pump).
